category_title: Anxious children who live in isolation and are reticent
minimal_context: The student is asked to put away a toy and join the large class for a lesson.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Class is starting. Please put your play items away and sit quietly with the rest of the children.

STUDENT: (Looks at the large group of children, holds onto a small wooden block, and remains completely quiet.)

TUTOR: Playing is for free time. Put the block down and join the group.

STUDENT: (Slowly steps backward away from the teacher, keeping their head down and not saying a word.)

TUTOR: You are not following the rules. Come here and sit down right now.

STUDENT: (Retreats further into the corner to hide, crouching down in isolation with an unhappy, fearful expression, completely withdrawing from the interaction.)

options.A: Create a behavior contract where the student receives a preferred toy for earning five green stickers, requiring 100% compliance with joining the group.
options.B: Break the transition goal into sub-targets: first week, join the large group for 10 minutes; second week, join for 90 minutes.
options.C: Conduct functional behavior analyses targeting the withdrawal behaviors and subject them to data-based hypothesis testing over time during classroom transitions.
options.D: Establish high-quality teacher-child relationships and maintain high teacher sensitivity over time during classroom transitions.
